properties.headline: Special Weather Statement issued July 21 at 7:01PM MDT by NWS Grand Junction CO
properties.description:
At 700 PM MDT, Doppler radar was tracking a strong thunderstorm 20
miles south of Grand Junction, moving west at 15 mph.

HAZARD...Wind gusts up to 50 mph and nickel size hail.

SOURCE...Radar indicated.

IMPACT...Gusty winds could knock down tree limbs and blow around
unsecured objects. Minor hail damage to vegetation and
outdoor objects is possible.

This storm will remain over mainly rural areas of west central Mesa
County.

This includes Colorado 141 between mile markers 120 and 135.
properties.areaDesc: Uncompahgre Plateau/Dallas Divide; Paradox Valley/Lower Dolores River

properties.headline: Flash Flood Warning issued July 21 at 6:57PM MDT until July 21 at 8:30PM MDT by NWS Grand Junction CO
properties.description:
At 657 PM MDT, Doppler radar indicated thunderstorms producing heavy
rain across the warned area. Over and inch of rain fell across the
area. Flash flooding is ongoing or expected to begin shortly.

HAZARD...Life threatening flash flooding. Thunderstorms producing
flash flooding.

SOURCE...Radar.

IMPACT...Life threatening flash flooding of creeks and streams,
urban areas, highways, streets and underpasses.

Some locations that will experience flash flooding include...
mainly rural areas of Western Garfield County

properties.headline: Flash Flood Warning issued July 21 at 6:40PM MDT until July 21 at 7:45PM MDT by NWS Grand Junction CO
properties.description:
FFWGJT

The National Weather Service in Grand Junction has extended the

* Flash Flood Warning for...
The Ferris burn scar in...
Northwestern Dolores County in southwestern Colorado...
Northern Montezuma County in southwestern Colorado...
West Central San Miguel County in southwestern Colorado...

* Until 745 PM MDT.

* At 640 PM MDT, Doppler radar indicated thunderstorms producing
heavy rain over the Ferris Burn Scar. Between 0.4 and 0.7 inches
of rain have fallen across the northwest portion of the Ferris
Burn Scar. The expected rainfall rate is 3 to 5 inches in 1 hour.
Flash flooding is ongoing or expected to begin shortly.

Excessive rainfall over the burn scar will result in debris flows.
The debris flow can consist of rock, mud, vegetation and other loose
materials.

HAZARD...Life threatening flash flooding. Thunderstorms
producing flash flooding in and around the Ferris Burn
Scar.

SOURCE...Radar.

IMPACT...Life threatening flash flooding of areas in and around
the Ferris Burn Scar.

* Some locations that will experience flash flooding include...
mainly rural areas of Northwestern Dolores, Northern Montezuma and
West Central San Miguel Counties
properties.areaDesc: Dolores, CO; Montezuma, CO; San Miguel, CO

properties.headline: Flash Flood Warning issued July 21 at 6:18PM MDT until July 21 at 7:30PM MDT by NWS Grand Junction CO
properties.description:
FFWGJT

The National Weather Service in Grand Junction has extended the

* Flash Flood Warning for...
Northeastern San Miguel County in southwestern Colorado...
Southern Montrose County in west central Colorado...

* Until 730 PM MDT.

* At 618 PM MDT, Doppler radar indicated thunderstorms producing
heavy rain in Highway 145 between Norwood Hill and Placerville.
Between 0.6 and 0.8 inches of rain have fallen. Flash flooding is
already occurring with multiple mudslides reported around 3:45 pm
MDT along Highway 145 between Norwood Hill and Placerville
resulting in a closure of Highway 145 between mile markers 85 and
96.

HAZARD...Life threatening flash flooding. Thunderstorms
producing flash flooding.

SOURCE...Radar.

IMPACT...Life threatening flash flooding of creeks and streams,
urban areas, highways, streets and underpasses.

* Some locations that will experience flash flooding include...
Norwood and Placerville, including areas along Highway 145.

properties.headline: Flood Advisory issued July 21 at 6:01PM MDT until July 21 at 9:00PM MDT by NWS Denver CO
properties.description:
* WHAT...Small stream flooding caused by excessive rainfall is
expected.

* WHERE...North Central Park County in central Colorado...

* WHEN...Until 900 PM MDT.

* IMPACTS...Rises in small streams and normally dry creeks.

* ADDITIONAL DETAILS...

- At 601 PM MDT, Doppler radar indicated heavy rain due to
thunderstorms. This may cause small stream flooding. Between 0.5
and 1.5 inches of rain have fallen.

- Additional rainfall amounts up to 0.5 inches are expected over
the area. This additional rain will result in minor flooding.

- Some locations that will experience flooding include...
Jefferson, Kenosha Pass and Tarryall Reservoir.

properties.headline: Flood Advisory issued July 21 at 5:55PM MDT until July 21 at 9:00PM MDT by NWS Grand Junction CO
properties.description:
* WHAT...Flooding caused by excessive rainfall is expected.

* WHERE...A portion of northwest Colorado, including the following
county, Garfield.

* WHEN...Until 900 PM MDT.

* IMPACTS...Minor flooding in low-lying and poor drainage areas.

* ADDITIONAL DETAILS...
- At 555 PM MDT, Doppler radar indicated heavy rain due to
thunderstorms. Minor flooding is ongoing or expected to begin
shortly in the advisory area.
- Some locations that will experience flooding include...
Parachute and Battlement Mesa.
- http://www.weather.gov/safety/flood

properties.headline: Flood Advisory issued July 21 at 5:40PM MDT until July 21 at 8:45PM MDT by NWS Grand Junction CO
properties.description:
* WHAT...Flash flooding and debris flows are possible, flooding
caused by excessive rainfall is expected.

* WHERE...A portion of southwest Colorado, including the following
county, Ouray.

* WHEN...Until 845 PM MDT.

* IMPACTS...Minor flooding in low-lying and poor drainage areas.

* ADDITIONAL DETAILS...
- At 540 PM MDT, Doppler radar indicated heavy rain due to
thunderstorms. Minor flooding is ongoing or expected to begin
shortly in the advisory area.
- Some locations that will experience flooding include...
Ouray.
- http://www.weather.gov/safety/flood

properties.headline: Flash Flood Warning issued July 21 at 5:06PM MDT until July 21 at 8:15PM MDT by NWS Grand Junction CO
properties.description:
FFWGJT

The National Weather Service in Grand Junction has issued a

* Flash Flood Warning for...
West Central Dolores County in southwestern Colorado...
Northwestern Montezuma County in southwestern Colorado...

* Until 815 PM MDT.

* At 506 PM MDT, Doppler radar indicated thunderstorms producing
heavy rain across the warned area. Between 1 and 2 inches of rain
have fallen. The expected rainfall rate is 2 to 4 inches in 1
hour. Flash flooding is ongoing or expected to begin shortly.

HAZARD...Life threatening flash flooding. Thunderstorms
producing flash flooding.

SOURCE...Radar.

IMPACT...Life threatening flash flooding of creeks and streams,
urban areas, highways, streets and underpasses.

* Some locations that will experience flash flooding include...
Cahone.

properties.headline: Flood Advisory issued July 21 at 4:56PM MDT until July 21 at 8:00PM MDT by NWS Denver CO
properties.description:
* WHAT...Small stream flooding caused by excessive rainfall is
expected.

* WHERE...West Central Larimer County in north central Colorado...

* WHEN...Until 800 PM MDT.

* IMPACTS...Rises in small streams and normally dry creeks.

* ADDITIONAL DETAILS...

- At 453 PM MDT, Doppler radar indicated heavy rain due to
thunderstorms. This may cause small stream flooding. Between 0.5
and 1 inch of rain has fallen.

- This includes the following streams and drainages...
Rawah Creek, Sheep Creek, North Fork Rawah Creek, Camp Creek,
Nunn Creek, Laramie River, Joe Wright Creek, Deadman Creek, Two
and One Half Creek, Brinker Creek and North Fork West Branch
Laramie River. Additional rainfall amounts of 0.5 to 1 inch are
expected over the area. This additional rain will result in minor
flooding.

- Some locations that will experience flooding include...
Mainly rural areas of West Central Larimer County.

properties.headline: Flash Flood Warning issued July 21 at 4:22PM MDT until July 21 at 7:30PM MDT by NWS Grand Junction CO
properties.description:
FFWGJT

The National Weather Service in Grand Junction has issued a

* Flash Flood Warning for...
Southwestern Dolores County in southwestern Colorado...
North Central Montezuma County in southwestern Colorado...

* Until 730 PM MDT.

* At 422 PM MDT, Doppler radar indicated thunderstorms producing
heavy rain across the warned area. Between 0.6 and 1 inch of rain
has fallen. The expected rainfall rate is 2 to 4 inches in 1 hour.
Flash flooding is ongoing or expected to begin shortly.

HAZARD...Life threatening flash flooding. Thunderstorms
producing flash flooding.

SOURCE...Radar.

IMPACT...Life threatening flash flooding of creeks and streams,
urban areas, highways, streets and underpasses.

* Some locations that will experience flash flooding include...
Lewis, Lebanon, Arriola and Yellow Jacket, including areas along
Highway 491 between Cahone and Cortez.
